allow:
Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Extend model to support mixed-type multivariate longitudinal data

Add support for mixed variable types \(continuous, binary, bounded\),
baseline covariates conditioning \(CVAE-style\), and landmark prediction
for forecasting future trajectories from partial observations.

New features:
- VariableConfig/VariableSpec for declaring variable types
- Type-aware normalization, loss computation \(MSE/BCE\), and EM imputation
- Baseline covariates concatenated to encoder/decoder \(conditional VAE\)
- predict_from_landmark\(\) on both LSTM and CNN models
- generate_mixed_longitudinal_data\(\) for synthetic mixed-type data
- mixed_vae_loss_function\(\) with per-type reconstruction losses

All 92 tests pass including 30 new tests for mixed-type functionality.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Update documentation for mixed-type multivariate support

- Add ARCHITECTURE.md explaining the project structure, what each file
  does, and where to start as a new user
- Update QUICKSTART.md with mixed-type data, baseline covariates, and
  landmark prediction examples
- Update tests/README_TESTS.md with the 30 new mixed-type tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Add mixed-type multivariate support to Julia implementation

Translate the Python mixed-type extensions to Julia/Flux:
- New config.jl: VariableSpec and VariableConfig for declaring
  continuous, binary, and bounded variable types
- Updated data.jl: type-aware normalization, baseline covariates,
  generate_mixed_longitudinal_data\(\), 4-tuple getindex
- Updated model.jl: baseline conditioning \(CVAE\) on both
  LongitudinalVAE and CNNLongitudinalVAE, per-variable output
  activations, predict_from_landmark, mixed_vae_loss with
  MSE for continuous and BCE for binary/bounded variables
- Updated trainer.jl: var_config support, 4-tuple unpacking,
  baseline passing, type-aware EM imputation
- Updated VAElong.jl: new exports for config, mixed loss,
  landmark prediction, and mixed data generation
- New mixed_type_example.jl demonstrating full workflow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Add GLMM and Seq2Seq LSTM benchmarks, fix noise variance collapse, add early stopping

- Add GLMM \(per-variable linear mixed model with BLUP\) and Seq2Seq LSTM
  benchmarks to examples for landmark prediction comparison
- Fix learned noise variance collapse: tighter clamp [-4, 2] + L2 penalty
  to anchor log_noise_var near 0 \(sigma^2 ~ 1 on normalized data\)
- Add early stopping with patience parameter to VAETrainer.fit\(\) — monitors
  validation loss and restores best model weights
- Fix plt.show\(\) blocking in non-interactive script mode \(-> plt.close\(\)\)
- Fix Unicode encoding error on Windows \(sigma character in cp1252\)
- Add statsmodels>=0.14.0 dependency for mixed models
- Mirror all changes in Julia implementation \(model, trainer, example\)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")
